The question aims to find out the total number of top-tier items (gold, silver, and bronze medals) that were both distributed and undistributed among all the nations listed in the table. We need to look at the "total" row (row 16) to get the answer, as it provides the sum of all medals.

column value of the question link to columns:

semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
distributed and undistributed -> total

The table shows that there were 45 gold, 49 silver, and 42 bronze items awarded, totaling 136. Since the question asks about both distributed and undistributed top-tier items, we must consider all the medals listed (gold, silver, bronze). All the medals shown in the table were distributed (since they have counts), and there is no data indicating undistributed items. However, if we consider the total column as including both distributed and undistributed items, then the answer would be based on the total count provided (136).
